plugin.name: discord_rich_presence.dll · cross-gen, old-gen + Next-Gen
plugin.description:
One F4SE plugin DLL drops into Data\F4SE\Plugins\ and loads on Fallout 4 1.10.163 (old-gen) and every Next-Gen build (1.10.984 / 1.11.169 / 1.11.191). The plugin auto-detects the runtime and resolves addresses from the right table.
After this installs the manager will have copied:
Data\F4SE\Plugins\discord_rich_presence.dll Data\F4SE\Plugins\discord_rich_presence.ini — default configuration Data\F4SE\Plugins\discord_rich_presence_translation.ini — translatable labels

plugin.name: 1. Discord desktop must be running
plugin.description:
The plugin talks to the Discord desktop client over a named pipe. The web client does not expose RPC, so the Rich Presence card will not appear without the desktop app open and logged in.
Discord → Settings → Activity Privacy → "Share your detected activities with others" must be ON for the card to render to anyone other than yourself.

plugin.name: 2. Set your Discord AppID in the INI
plugin.description:
The default AppID points at a placeholder app that only ships the large image. To see the custom small images (Pip-Boy, VATS, combat, etc.) you must:
1. Create your own application at https://discord.com/developers/applications 2. Upload the art assets shipped under dist\discord-assets\ to Rich Presence → Art Assets 3. Open Data\F4SE\Plugins\discord_rich_presence.ini 4. Under [Main] set: AppID = <your-19-digit-application-id> 5. Launch Fallout 4
Full step-by-step guide: docs\discord-app-setup.md in the repository.

plugin.name: 3. Customise the layout in the INI
plugin.description:
The full presence layout is data, not code. Open the same INI and edit:
[Format] field templates and separators (uses {name} {level} {hp} {caps} {location} {enemy} {menu} {event} tokens) [Images] per-state small-image keys (Pip-Boy, VATS, combat, explore, menu, mainmenu, loading) [Buttons] up to two clickable profile buttons (label + url)
The README has the full token list and key reference.
